_doc.t510_canonical_core: T510: Augment (Auggie CLI) reads lifecycle hooks from the `hooks` object in `.augment/settings.json` (Claude-Code-style: per-event array of { matcher, hooks:[{type:command,command,timeout}] }). IMPORTANT: Augment's `command` MUST be a path to a script with a supported extension (.ps1/.cmd/.bat/.sh) -- a bare `python x.py` is NOT accepted. So each native event points at a thin `.ps1` shim `g-hk-augment-<event>.ps1` that pipes the STDIN payload to the canonical entrypoint `python .augment/hooks/g-hk-on-<event>.py`, which calls `g_hk_core.dispatch("<event>")`. The shared core `.augment/hooks/g_hk_core.py` is byte-identical across every platform overlay and runs the per-event CONCERN_CHAIN (concern hooks self-gate on payload). Native->canonical map (g_hk_core.PLATFORM_EVENT_MAP['augment']): SessionStart->session-start, SessionEnd->session-end, PreToolUse->tool-start, PostToolUse->tool-end, Stop->stop. matcher is optional for PreToolUse/PostToolUse (defaults to .*); not used for session/stop events. timeout is in milliseconds. Schema verified against https://docs.augmentcode.com/cli/hooks .
_doc.unix_note: T510: shims are PowerShell (.ps1), matching the kiro-cli precedent in this repo. On Unix, Auggie dispatches .ps1 via PowerShell -- ensure `pwsh` is installed, or add parallel .sh shims if a POSIX-only install is required (follow-up).
